# Purpose
The JSON file contains a list of Unicode code points. These code points represent specific characters in the Unicode standard, which is a character encoding system used to support text in different languages and scripts. The file provides a narrow functionality by defining a specific set of characters that the application can recognize or process. This list is relevant to the codebase as it may be used to validate, filter, or transform text data within the application, ensuring that only the specified characters are handled. The inclusion of these code points can help in tasks such as text rendering, data validation, or localization.
# Content Summary
The provided JSON file contains a list of Unicode code points under the key "codepoints". These code points represent specific characters in the Unicode standard. The list includes a range of code points from 32 to 317, which cover basic Latin characters, punctuation, and some extended Latin characters. Additionally, the list includes higher code points such as 8217, 23545, 23565, and 37197, which correspond to specific characters outside the basic Latin range, possibly including typographic symbols or characters from other scripts.

Developers working with this file should understand that it defines a set of characters that may be used or supported by a software application. The presence of these code points suggests that the application may need to handle text rendering, encoding, or processing tasks involving these characters. It is important to ensure that any text processing or display functions in the application can correctly interpret and render these Unicode characters.
